mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market cooling and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of supply-driven inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in real personal income growth erodes household purchasing power, forcing consumers to cut discretionary spending and reducing corporate revenue expectations, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually while RSAFS growth remains positive, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding index of industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion coincides with negative 20-day equity returns in >55% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during periods of monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS rising > 0.25% per month).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which spills over into broader economic optimism and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical significance (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above the 290.0 threshold, it confirms entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >50% of periods where CPIAUCSL stays above 290.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for nominal GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal predicts negative returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of supply-side bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 5-day returns in >65% of instances, or the signal fails to hold when real interest rates are rising rapidly.
